title: SPIKE: does a PreToolUse payload distinguish orchestrator from subagent?
action: Answer ONE question with a real payload capture: can a PreToolUse hook tell an orchestrator's tool call apart from a dispatched subagent's? The Verdict must be the literal token YES or NO alone on a line under '## Verdict'. CAPTURE SHAPE IS A WHITELIST, NOT A DUMP — this is the safety requirement, do not weaken it: the probe records ONLY (i) the payload's field NAMES, (ii) each value's TYPE, (iii) the verbatim values of hook_event_name and tool_name (closed vocabularies), and (iv) sha256(session_id) truncated to 8 chars, so top-level and subagent captures can be compared for EQUALITY without recording the id. Never write a raw value: cwd and transcript_path are absolute home paths, transcript_path also embeds the session uuid and the repo path, and tool_input.file_path would record the path of any secret-shaped Read that the privacy guard DENIES (the write-guard and this probe are both in PreToolUse and both run). The whitelist still answers the question: a discriminator shows up as a key-set difference or as a differing session hash. BINDING: bind the probe in .claude/settings.local.json (git-ignored), NEVER in .claude/settings.json (tracked) — a tracked hook entry pointing at a gitignored .bee/spikes/ path would ENOENT on every Read in every other clone of this repo, forever. TEARDOWN IS MANDATORY: the binding is removed before this cell caps; a probe left bound logs every Read of every future session. HOOK SAFETY: wrap everything in try/catch, force process.exitCode = 0 unconditionally (a PreToolUse exit 2 BLOCKS every Read in the session), write nothing to stdout, and mkdirSync the capture directory recursively before appending. Claude Code snapshots hooks at session start, so the capture requires a fresh session — if that cannot be arranged inside this cell, return [BLOCKED] naming it as a human step; do not fake the finding.

truths: the spike file contains a '## Verdict' heading followed by a line that is exactly YES or exactly NO

either two captured payloads are diffed, OR the spike records the verbatim evidence that no subagent-originated invocation was captured at all, with the capture file contents shown — that absence IS the finding, and it caps the cell
the capture contains no raw cwd, no raw transcript_path, no raw session_id, and no file_path value
the probe binding is removed from .claude/settings.local.json before cap
the probe hook forces exit 0 on every path and writes nothing to stdout

artifacts.substantive: question, the whitelisted capture(s), the diff or the documented absence, and a bare YES/NO verdict

prohibitions: Never bind the probe in .claude/settings.json — it is tracked, and a hook pointing at a gitignored spike path breaks Read in every other clone
Never dump a raw payload — the capture is a whitelist of field names and value types, per the action
Never leave the probe bound at cap
The probe hook lives under .bee/spikes/ and is never promoted to hooks/ or registered in hooks/catalog.mjs
Do not build the tools logger in this cell
Do not paste an absolute home path or a session uuid into any tracked file — the decision log's secret patterns do not catch either

trace.outcome: Spike answered: real PreToolUse captures were obtained (whitelist-only: field names, types, verbatim hook_event_name/tool_name, sha256(session_id).slice(0,8)) but only from the subagent-dispatch side — this worker's own Read calls and a nested Explore subagent's Read, all byte-identical in field-set and session_hash8. The anticipated 'hooks snapshot at session start' blocker did NOT hold: a mid-session .claude/settings.local.json binding fired immediately. What DID block the comparison is structural: a worker cannot execute a tool call as the true top-level orchestrator from inside its own delegated turn, so no never-dispatched capture was obtainable. Per the cell's own must-haves, that single-sided documented absence is the finding. Verdict: NO.
trace.verify_output: EXIT:0 (all three checks passed). grep -A2 Verdict output: '## Verdict' then blank line then 'NO'. No /home/ or raw transcript_path value found in the spike file.
